title: salix trabzonica
intro: Salix trabzonica, also known as the Turkish white willow, is a species of willow tree native to Turkey and surrounding regions. The bark of Salix trabzonica contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used for its medicinal properties to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The health benefits of Salix trabzonica include its ability to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, lower fever, and soothe digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Salix trabzonica include teas, tinctures, and salves made from its bark, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
